general_notes: Standard thermodynamic properties at 298 K and 1 bar (NIST WebBook / NIST-JANAF). Each reaction requires applying Hess's law: ΔH°rxn = Σ νᵢ ΔHf°(products) − Σ νⱼ ΔHf°(reactants). log10(Keq) is derived from ΔG°rxn = Σ νᵢ ΔGf° via K = 10^(−ΔG°rxn / (RT ln10)), where RT ln10 = 5.708 kJ/mol at 298 K. Use gas-phase (g) formation values for all species unless the reaction specifies otherwise (e.g. C(s,graphite)). ΔHf° and ΔGf° of elements in their standard state are zero by definition.

search_groups.description: Chemical reaction equation with phase labels.

Standard enthalpy of reaction ΔH°rxn and equilibrium constant Keq at 298 K. Requires looking up ΔHf° and ΔGf° for each species from NIST WebBook or NIST-JANAF tables, then applying Hess's law and the Gibbs–Helmholtz relation.

validation_targets.description: Chemical reaction equation with stoichiometry and phase labels (g = gas, s = solid).
validation_targets.notes: Use the exact reaction string including phase labels as written in the CSV.

validation_targets.description: Standard molar enthalpy of reaction at 298 K in kJ/mol, computed via Hess's law from NIST standard enthalpies of formation.
validation_targets.notes: Sign convention: negative = exothermic. Apply Hess's law: ΔH°rxn = Σ νᵢ ΔHf°(i). Round to 1 decimal place. Source: NIST WebBook standard formation enthalpies at 298 K.

validation_targets.description: Base-10 logarithm of the thermodynamic equilibrium constant at 298 K, derived from ΔG°rxn = Σ νᵢ ΔGf°(i) and K = 10^(−ΔG°rxn / 5.708 kJ/mol).
validation_targets.notes: Use RT ln(10) = 5.708 kJ/mol at 298 K. Large negative values (e.g. −29.9) mean reaction is thermodynamically unfavorable at 298 K. Round to 2 decimal places. Source: NIST WebBook standard Gibbs formation energies at 298 K.
